deep_dive.title: Domain Arithmetic: One-Shot VLA Adaptation under Environmental Shifts
deep_dive.abstract: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models often fail to perform the same learned tasks under environmental shifts, such as changes in camera pose and shifts to a different but similar robot (e.g., from Panda to UR5e). Adapting these models to the shifted environment (i.e., target domain) often requires training on multiple demonstrations for each task, which are costly to collect. To reduce the burden of data curation and training, we propose an analogy-based method that adapts VLA models under environmental shifts through weight vector arithmetic with domain-specific information addition, named Domain ARiThmetic (DART). Unlike prior approaches, DART requires collecting only a single demonstration, enabling efficient adaptation. To accurately isolate domain-specific information for addition, DART performs subspace alignment between singular components in weight vectors to filter out noisy components. In both simulated and real-world experiments, DART outperforms existing VLA adaptation methods in one-shot scenarios across diverse visual and embodiment shifts. Code is available at https://github.com/snumprlab/dart.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: proposes one-shot domain adaptation for vision-language-action models
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出面向视觉-语言-动作模型的单次域适应方法
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出面向视觉-语言-动作模型的单次域适应方法
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出面向视觉-语言-动作模型的单次域适应方法
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Domain Arithmetic (DART) adapts VLA models to new environments using weight vector arithmetic with domain-specific information from a single demonstration, enabling efficient one-shot adaptation under environmental shifts such as camera pose change and robot variation.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 论文提出域算术方法DART，通过权重向量算术注入领域特定信息，利用单个演示实现视觉-语言-动作模型在环境偏移（如相机位姿变化、机器人类型差异）下的高效适应。该方法显著降低了数据收集成本，在多个场景中取得了与多演示方法相当的适应性能。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 论文提出域算术方法DART，通过权重向量算术注入领域特定信息，利用单个演示实现视觉-语言-动作模型在环境偏移（如相机位姿变化、机器人类型差异）下的高效适应。该方法显著降低了数据收集成本，在多个场景中取得了与多演示方法相当的适应性能。
